title: Podman hangs when using --tty argument with interactive images like python, node
body:
### Issue Description

Podman hangs when using --tty argument with interactive images like python, node

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run `podman run -t python:3.12` or `:3.9`, or `node:21.5.0-bullseye`



### Describe the results you received

The stdin and stdout hangs an no characters can be submitted to the pods REPL

### Describe the results you expected

The Node and Python REPLs should function

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional environment details

uname -a

```

$ dpkg -l podman
Desired=Unknown/Install/Remove/Purge/Hold
| Status=Not/Inst/Conf-files/Unpacked/halF-conf/Half-inst/trig-aWait/Trig-pend
|/ Err?=(none)/Reinst-required (Status,Err: uppercase=bad)
||/ Name           Version                    Architecture Description
+++-==============-==========================-============-==========================================
ii  podman         3.4.4+ds1-1ubuntu1.22.04.2 amd64        engine to run OCI-based containers in Pods
```

### Additional information

When running `podman run --rm --name=python -t python:latest` I can run `podman attach python` in another terminal to get a functioning REPL, all stdout is reproduced in the original command. Pressing `Ctrl+D` exits both interactive processes simultaneously.

comments.body:
I am using the following command as a workaround

```bash
podman attach $(podman run --rm --detach -t python:latest)
```
Simliar issues exist with the `podman exec` command, e.g. `podman exec <container_id> ipython`

Use `--interactive` and `--tty` together, e.g. `podman exec -it 34383b53d742 ipython`, `podman run --rm -it python:latest`.

https://access.redhat.com/documentation/en-us/red_hat_enterprise_linux/8/html-single/building_running_and_managing_containers/index#con_podman-run-command_assembly_working-with-containers
